questions.question: What is the output of `"hello" == "hel" + "lo"`?
options: true — compile-time constant folding puts both in the String pool
Compilation error — cannot use == with Strings
true — all String concatenations are interned
questions.explanation: The compiler performs constant folding for compile-time string concatenation. Both "hello" and "hel" + "lo" resolve to the same pool reference at compile time.

questions.question: Which method should you prefer for trimming whitespace in modern Java?
options: trim() — it handles all whitespace types
strip() — it is Unicode-aware and handles all whitespace characters
questions.explanation: strip() (Java 11+) uses Character.isWhitespace() which is Unicode-aware, handling characters like \u2003 (em space) that trim() misses.

questions.question: What happens when you call `new StringBuilder("abc").insert(1, "XY").toString()`?
questions.explanation: insert(1, "XY") inserts the string at index 1, pushing existing characters to the right: 'a' + 'XY' + 'bc' = "aXYbc".

questions.question: Which is a valid text block in Java 21?
questions.explanation: The opening """ must be followed by a newline — no content on the same line. Option C has text after the opening """, which is a compile error. Option D uses single quotes which is not valid Java.

questions.question: What does `"Java 21".formatted()` do if called with no arguments?
options: Compilation error — formatted() requires at least one argument
questions.explanation: formatted() with no arguments works like String.format() with no arguments — it returns the original string unchanged since there are no format specifiers to process.
